title: Volunteer task competency boundary
summary: Match each task to training, physical ability, protective equipment, supervision, legal limits, and the right to refuse.
content: Match each task to training, physical ability, protective equipment, supervision, legal limits, and the right to refuse. Use untrained volunteers for low-risk support while specialists handle medical, electrical, gas, structural, chemical, and rescue work. Do not reward risky improvisation or punish someone for reporting a hazard.
materials: Notebook or printed card. Context: “Volunteer task competency boundary”.
Relevant labels, contacts, and official alerts — for «Volunteer task competency boundary»
steps: Match each task to training, physical ability, protective equipment, supervision, legal limits, and the right to refuse.
Use untrained volunteers for low-risk support while specialists handle medical, electrical, gas, structural, chemical, and rescue work.
Assign one person to act and another to verify the result for Volunteer task competency boundary.
Record time, source, action, result, unresolved risk, and next review for Volunteer task competency boundary.
warnings: Do not reward risky improvisation or punish someone for reporting a hazard.
Official instructions and trained responders override this offline card for Volunteer task competency boundary.
common_mistakes: Acting before location, time, hazard, and responsible person are clear for Volunteer task competency boundary.
Failing to update the plan when conditions change during Volunteer task competency boundary.
alternatives: Use a paper note, SMS, radio message, or trusted messenger when the main system is unavailable for Volunteer task competency boundary.
failure_signs: The decision source, responsible person, or next review cannot be identified for Volunteer task competency boundary.
The action creates a new hazard or delays qualified help during Volunteer task competency boundary.
when_not_to_use: Do not use this card to override an evacuation order, emergency responder, clinician, product label, or local law for Volunteer task competency boundary.
short_term: For Volunteer task competency boundary, stabilize the immediate risk, communicate the decision, and schedule the next check before attention moves elsewhere.
long_term: After Volunteer task competency boundary, update supplies, contact lists, maps, training, and the written plan from what actually happened.
if_method_fails: Stop Volunteer task competency boundary, isolate the unsafe result, communicate the failure, and use the safest official or professionally supported fallback available.
environment_notes: Adapt Volunteer task competency boundary to Greek heat, wildfire smoke, earthquakes, islands, mountain roads, apartment stairs, power cuts, water interruptions, mobility limits, and official alerts.
sources: CDC — Water, sanitation and hygiene emergencies: https://www.cdc.gov/water-emergency/index.html
CDC — Keep food safe after an emergency: https://www.cdc.gov/food-safety/foods/keep-food-safe-after-emergency.html
American Red Cross — First aid steps: https://www.redcross.org/take-a-class/first-aid/performing-first-aid/first-aid-steps
